mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered quarters or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant at p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during late-cycle expansion phases reflects peak manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during late-cycle phases or 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 65% of acceleration periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real corporate profit margins and trigger expectations of tighter monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 60% of months with CPIAUCSL increases or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 200,000 in a single month confirm robust economic expansion and rising aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months where payroll growth surpasses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures validates broad-based economic recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 60% of quarters or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0% during a tightening cycle, higher discount rates and reduced liquidity expectations compress equity valuations, generating negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 65% of periods where the funds rate remains above 2.0% during tightening phases.
